teachingPoint: Webflow Designer Extension uses OAuth 2.0 authorization via Webflow API. Token obtained via fabric, not hardcoded. Token stored in secrets fabric with 1-hour TTL. Service refreshes token transparently on 401 responses from Webflow API.
positiveExample: T666 WebflowDesignFetcher calls fabric.getWebflowToken() which handles OAuth dance and token refresh. On 401, fabric re-authenticates and retries. Service logic never sees OAuth details.
negativeExample: Hardcoded API token in config. No token refresh. Expiration causes permanent 401 until restart. OAuth logic mixed into service code.
discriminatingConstraint: OAuth token obtained via ISecretsFabric only. Automatic refresh on 401 response. Token never logged or exposed. TTL enforced at fabric layer.

teachingPoint: Webflow uses nested component hierarchy. XIIGen normalizes to flat element list with parentId references. Recursive traversal at ORDER 1 validates cycle-free structure. Max depth 50 to prevent stack overflow.
positiveExample: T666 calls fabric.getWebflowComponent(componentId). If nested, recursively fetches children with depth tracking. Cycle detection: if parentId chain returns to self, returns CyclicComponentHierarchy error.
negativeExample: Trusting Webflow component tree directly without cycle checking. Infinite recursion on circular parent-child refs. No depth limit.
discriminatingConstraint: Recursive traversal with cycle detection at ORDER 1. Max depth 50. Parentless components valid (roots). Cycles return error without traversing.

teachingPoint: Webflow CSS selector extraction: components reference CSS selectors for styling. XIIGen normalizes to class list. Selector parsing at ORDER 2 handles compound selectors (e.g., '.container .child') by extracting rightmost class as canonical selector.
positiveExample: T666 parses selector '.container .child' → extract rightmost: '.child'. Store as classes: ['container', 'child']. On export, reconstruct appropriate format.
negativeExample: Storing selectors as-is. Cannot port to non-CSS targets (e.g., Figma). No normalization.
discriminatingConstraint: Selector parsing at ORDER 2. Compound selectors split into class list. Class names extracted and canonicalized. Unknown selectors logged but don't block processing.

teachingPoint: Multi-frame project handling: Webflow projects contain multiple frames (pages/sections). Frame context must be tracked throughout processing. Each element belongs to single frame. Frame-scoped queries at ORDER 1 prevent cross-frame element conflicts.
positiveExample: T666 stores frameId with each element: { elementId, frameId, componentId }. Queries filter by frameId at storage time. Export targets correct frame.
negativeExample: Elements stored without frameId. Same componentId on multiple frames cause collisions. Cross-frame access possible.
discriminatingConstraint: frameId stored with every element. All queries include frameId predicate. Element access cross-frame returns NotFound.

description: OAuth 2.0 token management via secrets fabric. Automatic refresh on 401. TTL enforcement. No hardcoded tokens. Token never exposed in logs.
Nested component hierarchy → flat element list with parentId references. Recursive traversal with cycle detection (max depth 50). Prevents infinite recursion.
CSS selector extraction and class normalization. Compound selectors parsed into class lists. Rightmost class extracted as canonical selector. Enables cross-platform export.
Frame-scoped element isolation. Each element stores frameId. Frame-scoped queries prevent cross-frame collisions. Multi-frame projects handled correctly.
